title: Hook wiring plus the contract-test split the capsule requires
action: Per D5/D6/D9/D10/D11/D16/D18. Wire the hooks as thin callers of compaction.mjs — no logic in the hooks themselves (D3). bee-session-init.mjs: on SessionStart with source=compact, emit buildCompactCapsule instead of buildSessionPreamble, and append the resume record; every other source keeps today's full preamble byte-identical (D8); ADOPT_SOURCES and the handoff ADOPTION path are untouched, and buildSessionPreamble keeps rendering its own handoff block — the capsule re-renders it, it does not move it. bee-session-close.mjs: on PreCompact, append the precompact record, render the D9 survival warning when cell_compact_count >= 2, and emit the anchor nudge with force:true bypassing dedup (matching maybeCaptureQueueNudge at :413-415) — all through emitHookOutput as systemMessage, NEVER encodeBlock, per the B2/R14 contract at :234-238. bee-prompt-context.mjs: emit the deduped anchor nudge, key 'anchor-missing-nudge', hash <sessionId>:<feature>:<cell>, via the existing shouldInject/markInjected. Then the D16 split: hooks/test_hook_contracts.mjs:2740-2780 currently loops ['compact','resume'] asserting out.endsWith of the control preamble. Split it — resume keeps that byte-identical additive assertion UNCHANGED; compact gets a new assertion covering anchor-first ordering, the capsule's required items, and the ABSENCE of the startup-only sections. The compact branch must also REPLACE the ordering check at :2762 (indexOf of the verbatim request vs indexOf of '- Phase:'): indexOf returns -1 on a renamed label, so a naive port passes or fails for the wrong reason. Copy all three hooks to .bee/bin/hooks/ in this cell (D18).

truths: The resume half of the split assertion is unchanged and still green — ia-1's D4/D5 remains in force for that source.
The compact assertion fails if the capsule is deliberately broken — it must bite, not merely pass.
PreCompact output carries no turn-control verdict of any kind.
The two other compact-source assertions at :2427-2455 and :2854-2883 stay green, proving D6 item 4 kept the HANDOFF heading verbatim.
artifacts: Three wired hooks plus their .bee/bin/hooks/ mirrors
The split contract-test block with both branches
prohibitions: Never emit encodeBlock from PreCompact — the advisory contract forbids it and D9 depends on that.
Never let the 30-minute dedup swallow the PreCompact nudge; it is forced there.
Do not weaken the resume branch to make the compact branch easier.

trace.dropped_reason: Slice 1 re-shaped after validation returned NOT READY (6 blocking findings). Superseded by the D19-D22 shape: hook keeps anchor ownership, per-cell manifest+ledger regen, serial chain, split cells.
